sources.title: T Cell Markers and B Cell Markers

tissue_systems.name: Integumentary System
tissue_systems.description: The organ system that protects the body from damage, comprising the skin and its appendages.

organs.name: Skin
organs.description: The largest organ, serving as a barrier between the internal and external environment.

microstructures.name: Epidermis
microstructures.description: The outermost layer of the skin, composed of stratified squamous epithelium.

cell_types.name: Keratinocyte
cell_types.description: Predominant epidermal cells that differentiate from epidermal stem cells and produce keratin.

cell_types.name: Epidermal Stem Cell
cell_types.description: Slow-cycling stem cells in the basal layer and hair follicle bulge, responsible for epidermal renewal.

cell_types.name: Langerhans Cell
cell_types.description: Epidermal dendritic cells identified by Birbeck granules, involved in immune surveillance.

microstructures.name: Dermis
microstructures.description: The thick inner layer of skin containing connective tissue, blood vessels, and nerve endings.

cell_types.name: Dermal Fibroblast
cell_types.description: Connective tissue cells producing collagen and extracellular matrix in the dermis.

cell_types.name: Dermal Macrophage
cell_types.description: Tissue-resident macrophages in the dermis involved in clearance and immune homeostasis.

cell_types.name: Dermal Dendritic Cell
cell_types.description: Antigen-presenting cells in the dermis, distinct from Langerhans cells.

microstructures.name: Hypodermis (Subcutaneous Adipose)
microstructures.description: The deepest layer of skin composed primarily of adipose tissue.

cell_types.name: White Adipocyte
cell_types.description: Adipocytes that store energy as lipids. Express leptin and HOXC markers.

cell_types.name: Brown Adipocyte
cell_types.description: Thermogenic adipocytes expressing UCP1, involved in heat production.

cell_types.name: Beige Adipocyte
cell_types.description: UCP1-expressing adipocytes that develop within white adipose tissue.

cell_types.name: Adipose-Derived Stem Cell
cell_types.description: Pluripotent progenitor cells from adipose tissue.

tissue_systems.name: Nervous System
tissue_systems.description: The complex network of nerves and cells that transmit signals throughout the body.

organs.name: Brain
organs.description: The central organ of the nervous system, responsible for processing sensory information and controlling motor functions.

microstructures.name: Cerebral Cortex
microstructures.description: The outer layer of the cerebrum involved in higher-order brain functions.

cell_types.name: Pyramidal Cell
cell_types.description: Neurons in the cerebral cortex, hippocampus, and amygdala important for cognitive functions.

cell_types.name: Type 1 Astrocyte
cell_types.description: Protoplasmic astrocytes with specific marker expression.

cell_types.name: Type 2 Astrocyte
cell_types.description: Fibrous astrocytes with distinct marker expression from type 1.

microstructures.name: Cerebellum
microstructures.description: The brain region responsible for motor coordination and balance.

cell_types.name: Purkinje Cell
cell_types.description: Large neurons in the cerebellar cortex that output to deep cerebellar nuclei.

microstructures.name: Choroid Plexus
microstructures.description: Structure in the ventricles that produces cerebrospinal fluid.

cell_types.name: Choroid Plexus Epithelial Cell
cell_types.description: Epithelial cells of the choroid plexus that produce CSF.

microstructures.name: Brain Microglia
microstructures.description: Resident macrophages of the central nervous system derived from yolk sac progenitors.

cell_types.name: Microglia
cell_types.description: Resident immune cells of the CNS derived from yolk sac macrophages, involved in synaptic pruning and neuroinflammation.

organs.name: Spinal Cord
organs.description: A long tube of nervous tissue extending from the brain, carrying signals between brain and body.

microstructures.name: White Matter
microstructures.description: Myelinated nerve fiber tracts in the spinal cord.

cell_types.name: Oligodendrocyte
cell_types.description: Myelinating glial cells of the CNS that wrap axons with myelin sheaths.

microstructures.name: Gray Matter
microstructures.description: Neuronal cell bodies and synapses in the spinal cord.

cell_types.name: Ependymal Cell
cell_types.description: Epithelial cells lining the ventricles and central canal of the spinal cord.

organs.name: Peripheral Nerves
organs.description: Nerves outside the brain and spinal cord connecting the CNS to limbs and organs.

microstructures.name: Nerve Sheath
microstructures.description: Connective tissue and glial cell layers surrounding peripheral nerve fibers.

cell_types.name: Myelinating Schwann Cell
cell_types.description: Glial cells that wrap around peripheral axons forming myelin sheaths.

cell_types.name: Non-myelinating Schwann Cell
cell_types.description: Schwann cells that surround but do not myelinate small-diameter axons.

cell_types.name: Satellite Glial Cell
cell_types.description: Glial cells surrounding neuronal cell bodies in peripheral ganglia.

cell_types.name: Enteric Glial Cell
cell_types.description: Glial cells of the enteric nervous system that support gut neurons.

tissue_systems.name: Cardiovascular System
tissue_systems.description: The organ system that circulates blood throughout the body, comprising the heart and blood vessels.

organs.name: Heart
organs.description: A muscular organ that pumps blood through the circulatory system.

microstructures.name: Myocardium
microstructures.description: The muscular tissue of the heart wall responsible for contraction.

cell_types.name: Cardiomyocyte
cell_types.description: Muscle cells of the heart responsible for contractile function.

cell_types.name: Cardiac Macrophage
cell_types.description: Tissue-resident macrophages in the heart with subsets defined by CCR2, TIMD4, LYVE1, and MHC-II.

organs.name: Blood Vessels
organs.description: Tubular structures that carry blood throughout the body.

microstructures.name: Vessel Intima (Endothelium)
microstructures.description: The inner lining of blood vessels composed of endothelial cells.

cell_types.name: Vascular Endothelial Cell
cell_types.description: Cells forming the interior surface of blood vessels.

cell_types.name: Lymphatic Endothelial Cell
cell_types.description: Endothelial cells lining lymphatic vessels, distinct from vascular endothelium.

microstructures.name: Vessel Media
microstructures.description: The middle layer of blood vessels containing smooth muscle cells.

cell_types.name: Smooth Muscle Cell
cell_types.description: Muscle cells in the walls of hollow organs and blood vessels.

microstructures.name: Vessel Adventitia
microstructures.description: The outer connective tissue layer of blood vessels.

cell_types.name: Pericyte
cell_types.description: Cells located around capillaries and venules that help maintain vascular homeostasis.

tissue_systems.name: Respiratory System
tissue_systems.description: The organ system responsible for gas exchange, comprising the lungs and airways.

organs.name: Lung
organs.description: Paired organs responsible for gas exchange between air and blood.

microstructures.name: Alveoli
microstructures.description: Tiny air sacs where gas exchange occurs between air and blood.

cell_types.name: Type I Alveolar Epithelial Cell
cell_types.description: Thin squamous cells covering most of the alveolar surface for gas exchange.

cell_types.name: Type II Alveolar Epithelial Cell
cell_types.description: Cuboidal cells producing surfactant and serving as progenitors for AT1 cells.

cell_types.name: Alveolar Macrophage
cell_types.description: Resident macrophages on the alveolar surface that phagocytose surfactant and pathogens.

microstructures.name: Bronchi
microstructures.description: Large airways conducting air into the lungs.

cell_types.name: Bronchial Epithelial Cell
cell_types.description: Epithelial cells lining the bronchi, involved in mucociliary clearance.

cell_types.name: Pulmonary Neuroendocrine Cell
cell_types.description: Rare epithelial cells in the airways activated by neurotransmitters, releasing hormones.

tissue_systems.name: Digestive System
tissue_systems.description: The organ system responsible for food processing and nutrient absorption.

organs.name: Stomach
organs.description: A muscular organ that breaks down food using acid and enzymes.

microstructures.name: Gastric Glands
microstructures.description: Glands in the stomach lining that produce acid, enzymes, and hormones.

cell_types.name: Parietal Cell
cell_types.description: Stomach cells that secrete hydrochloric acid and intrinsic factor.

cell_types.name: Chief Cell
cell_types.description: Stomach cells that secrete pepsinogen.

organs.name: Small Intestine
organs.description: The primary site of nutrient absorption, divided into duodenum, jejunum, and ileum.

microstructures.name: Intestinal Villi
microstructures.description: Finger-like projections that increase the absorptive surface area.

cell_types.name: Enterocyte
cell_types.description: Intestinal absorptive cells responsible for absorption of water, ions, and nutrients.

microstructures.name: Intestinal Crypt (Crypt of Lieberkuhn)
microstructures.description: Glands at the base of villi containing stem cells and secretory cells.

cell_types.name: Paneth Cell
cell_types.description: Cells at the crypt base that secrete antimicrobial peptides and lysozyme.

cell_types.name: Intestinal Stem Cell
cell_types.description: Multipotent stem cells in the crypt base that continuously replenish the intestinal epithelium.

cell_types.name: Tuft Cell
cell_types.description: Specialized epithelial cells using taste receptors to detect pathogens, found in digestive and respiratory tracts.

cell_types.name: Goblet Cell
cell_types.description: Mucus-secreting epithelial cells in the intestinal lining.

organs.name: Large Intestine (Colon)
organs.description: The final section of the digestive tract, responsible for water absorption and waste formation.

microstructures.name: Colonic Epithelium
microstructures.description: The lining of the colon composed of absorptive and secretory cells.

cell_types.name: Colonocyte
cell_types.description: Absorptive epithelial cells of the colon.

organs.name: Liver
organs.description: The largest internal organ, responsible for metabolism, detoxification, and protein synthesis.

microstructures.name: Hepatic Lobule
microstructures.description: The functional unit of the liver, hexagonal in shape with a central vein.

cell_types.name: Hepatocyte
cell_types.description: Parenchymal cells of the liver responsible for metabolism, detoxification, and protein synthesis.

cell_types.name: Kupffer Cell
cell_types.description: Liver-resident macrophages derived from yolk sac progenitors, maintaining immunological tolerance.

microstructures.name: Hepatic Sinusoids
microstructures.description: Specialized capillaries in the liver connecting portal triads to the central vein.

cell_types.name: Liver Sinusoidal Endothelial Cell
cell_types.description: Specialized endothelial cells with fenestrations allowing plasma passage.

cell_types.name: Hepatic Stellate Cell
cell_types.description: Pericyte-like cells in the space of Disse that store vitamin A and regulate fibrosis.

organs.name: Pancreas
organs.description: A dual-function organ with exocrine and endocrine components.

microstructures.name: Pancreatic Acini
microstructures.description: Exocrine units producing digestive enzymes.

cell_types.name: Pancreatic Acinar Cell
cell_types.description: Exocrine cells producing digestive enzymes like amylase, trypsinogen, and lipase.

microstructures.name: Islets of Langerhans
microstructures.description: Endocrine cell clusters producing insulin, glucagon, and other hormones.

cell_types.name: Pancreatic Beta Cell
cell_types.description: Endocrine cells producing insulin, critical for glucose homeostasis.

cell_types.name: Pancreatic Alpha Cell
cell_types.description: Endocrine cells producing glucagon to raise blood glucose.

cell_types.name: Pancreatic Delta Cell
cell_types.description: Endocrine cells producing somatostatin.

tissue_systems.name: Lymphatic/Immune System
tissue_systems.description: The organ system responsible for immune defense and lymph circulation.

organs.name: Bone Marrow
organs.description: The soft tissue inside bones where blood cell production (hematopoiesis) occurs.

microstructures.name: Hematopoietic Niche
microstructures.description: The microenvironment supporting hematopoietic stem cell maintenance and differentiation.

cell_types.name: Hematopoietic Stem Cell
cell_types.description: Stem cells giving rise to all blood cell lineages.

cell_types.name: Neutrophil
cell_types.description: The most abundant granulocyte, first responders to bacterial infection.

cell_types.name: Eosinophil
cell_types.description: Granulocytes involved in allergic reactions and anti-parasitic defense.

cell_types.name: Basophil
cell_types.description: The rarest granulocyte, involved in allergic responses and parasitic defense.

cell_types.name: Classical Monocyte
cell_types.description: Inflammatory monocytes that patrol extravascular tissues and transport antigens.

cell_types.name: Non-classical Monocyte
cell_types.description: Patrolling monocytes that maintain vascular integrity.

cell_types.name: Mast Cell
cell_types.description: Granulated cells of hematopoietic origin containing histamine and heparin, key in allergy.

organs.name: Thymus
organs.description: A primary lymphoid organ where T cells mature.

microstructures.name: Thymic Cortex
microstructures.description: The outer region of the thymus where T cell positive selection occurs.

cell_types.name: Double-Negative Thymocyte
cell_types.description: Early T cell precursors lacking both CD4 and CD8, progressing through DN1-DN4 stages.

cell_types.name: Double-Positive Thymocyte
cell_types.description: Immature T cells expressing both CD4 and CD8, undergoing positive and negative selection.

microstructures.name: Thymic Medulla
microstructures.description: The inner region where negative selection of T cells occurs.

cell_types.name: Thymic Tuft Cell
cell_types.description: Rare epithelial cells in the thymus similar to intestinal tuft cells.

organs.name: Spleen
organs.description: A secondary lymphoid organ filtering blood and mounting immune responses.

microstructures.name: White Pulp
microstructures.description: Lymphoid tissue of the spleen containing T and B cell zones.

cell_types.name: Splenic Macrophage
cell_types.description: Macrophages in the spleen involved in filtering blood and immune responses.

organs.name: Lymph Nodes
organs.description: Secondary lymphoid organs filtering lymph and coordinating immune responses.

microstructures.name: Germinal Center
microstructures.description: Sites within lymph node follicles where B cells undergo affinity maturation.

cell_types.name: Follicular Helper T Cell
cell_types.description: CD4+ T cells that assist B cells in germinal centers with CXCR5-mediated homing.

cell_types.name: Follicular Regulatory T Cell
cell_types.description: Foxp3+ T cells in germinal centers that suppress B cell proliferation and TFH activity.

cell_types.name: Follicular Dendritic Cell
cell_types.description: Non-hematopoietic cells in germinal centers that retain antigens for B cell selection.

tissue_systems.name: Endocrine System
tissue_systems.description: The organ system of glands that secrete hormones directly into the bloodstream.

organs.name: Thyroid
organs.description: A butterfly-shaped gland in the neck producing thyroid hormones.

microstructures.name: Thyroid Follicle
microstructures.description: Spherical structures that produce and store thyroid hormones.

cell_types.name: Thyroid Follicular Cell
cell_types.description: Epithelial cells of thyroid follicles that produce T3 and T4.

organs.name: Adrenal Gland
organs.description: Paired glands above the kidneys producing cortisol, aldosterone, and adrenaline.

microstructures.name: Adrenal Cortex
microstructures.description: The outer layer producing steroid hormones (cortisol, aldosterone).

cell_types.name: Zona Glomerulosa Cell
cell_types.description: Cortical cells producing aldosterone for salt balance.

cell_types.name: Zona Fasciculata Cell
cell_types.description: Cortical cells producing cortisol in response to ACTH.

microstructures.name: Adrenal Medulla
microstructures.description: The inner layer producing catecholamines (adrenaline, noradrenaline).

cell_types.name: Chromaffin Cell
cell_types.description: Neuroendocrine cells of the adrenal medulla that secrete epinephrine and norepinephrine.

tissue_systems.name: Musculoskeletal System
tissue_systems.description: The organ system providing support, movement, and protection to the body.

organs.name: Skeletal Muscle
organs.description: Voluntary muscles attached to bones responsible for body movement.

microstructures.name: Muscle Fiber
microstructures.description: Multinucleated contractile cells forming skeletal muscle tissue.

cell_types.name: Skeletal Myocyte
cell_types.description: Multinucleated contractile cells of skeletal muscle.

cell_types.name: Muscle Satellite Cell
cell_types.description: Stem cells located between the basal lamina and sarcolemma of muscle fibers, responsible for muscle regeneration.

organs.name: Bone
organs.description: Rigid organs forming the skeleton, providing structure and protecting internal organs.

microstructures.name: Bone Marrow Cavity
microstructures.description: The central cavity of bones containing hematopoietic and stromal cells.

cell_types.name: Mesenchymal Stem Cell
cell_types.description: Multipotent stromal cells capable of differentiating into bone, cartilage, and fat.

cell_types.name: Osteoblast
cell_types.description: Bone-forming cells that secrete bone matrix.

cell_types.name: Osteoclast
cell_types.description: Large multinucleated cells that resorb bone tissue.

tissue_systems.name: Reproductive System
tissue_systems.description: The organ system responsible for reproduction.

organs.name: Ovary
organs.description: The female reproductive organ producing oocytes and hormones.

microstructures.name: Ovarian Follicle
microstructures.description: Structures containing an oocyte surrounded by granulosa and theca cells.

cell_types.name: Granulosa Cell
cell_types.description: Cells forming a barrier around ovarian oocyte follicles, producing estradiol and progesterone.

cell_types.name: Large Luteal Cell
cell_types.description: Cells in the corpus luteum producing progesterone and oxytocin, derived from granulosa cells.

cell_types.name: Germ Cell (Ovary)
cell_types.description: Cells involved in reproduction including oocytes and gonocytes.

organs.name: Testis
organs.description: The male reproductive organ producing sperm and testosterone.

microstructures.name: Seminiferous Tubule
microstructures.description: Coiled tubes in the testis where spermatogenesis occurs.

cell_types.name: Sertoli Cell
cell_types.description: Nurse cells of the testis that support spermatogenesis and control hormone transport.

cell_types.name: Germ Cell (Testis)
cell_types.description: Cells involved in sperm production including spermatogonia and spermatocytes.

organs.name: Prostate
organs.description: A gland in males producing seminal fluid.

microstructures.name: Prostate Epithelium
microstructures.description: The glandular epithelium of the prostate.

cell_types.name: Prostate Epithelial Cell
cell_types.description: Epithelial cells of the prostate gland producing prostate-specific antigen.

tissue_systems.name: Urinary System
tissue_systems.description: The organ system responsible for filtering blood and excreting waste as urine.

organs.name: Kidney
organs.description: Paired organs that filter blood, regulate electrolytes, and produce urine.

microstructures.name: Glomerulus
microstructures.description: The filtering unit of the kidney consisting of Bowman's capsule and capillary tuft.

cell_types.name: Podocyte
cell_types.description: Specialized epithelial cells with foot processes wrapping around glomerular capillaries.

cell_types.name: Mesangial Cell
cell_types.description: Specialized pericytes in the glomerulus providing structural support.

microstructures.name: Nephron Tubules
microstructures.description: Tubular structures that process the glomerular filtrate.

cell_types.name: Proximal Tubule Cell
cell_types.description: Epithelial cells of the proximal tubule responsible for reabsorption.

cell_types.name: Distal Tubule Cell
cell_types.description: Epithelial cells of the distal tubule involved in fine-tuning electrolyte balance.

tissue_systems.name: Sensory System
tissue_systems.description: The organ system responsible for detecting sensory stimuli from the environment.

organs.name: Eye
organs.description: The organ of vision.

microstructures.name: Retina
microstructures.description: The light-sensitive layer at the back of the eye.

cell_types.name: Retinal Ganglion Cell
cell_types.description: Output neurons of the retina that transmit visual information through the optic nerve.

tissue_systems.name: Circulating Immune Cells
tissue_systems.description: Immune cells circulating in blood and lymph, not confined to a single tissue.

organs.name: Blood
organs.description: Circulating fluid carrying cells, nutrients, and waste throughout the body.

microstructures.name: Blood Leukocytes
microstructures.description: White blood cells involved in immune defense.

cell_types.name: Naive CD4+ T Cell
cell_types.description: Mature CD4+ T cells that have not yet encountered their specific antigen.

cell_types.name: Naive CD8+ T Cell
cell_types.description: Mature CD8+ T cells that have not yet encountered their specific antigen.

cell_types.name: TH1 Cell
cell_types.description: CD4+ helper T cells producing IFN-gamma, activating macrophages against intracellular pathogens.

cell_types.name: TH2 Cell
cell_types.description: CD4+ helper T cells producing IL-4, IL-5, IL-13, mediating allergic and anti-parasitic responses.

cell_types.name: TH17 Cell
cell_types.description: CD4+ helper T cells producing IL-17, involved in autoimmunity and anti-fungal defense.

cell_types.name: Regulatory T Cell
cell_types.description: CD4+ T cells that suppress immune responses, maintaining self-tolerance.

cell_types.name: TH2A Cell
cell_types.description: A subset of TH2 cells associated with allergic disease.

cell_types.name: TH9 Cell
cell_types.description: CD4+ T cells secreting IL-9 and IL-10, with anti-tumor properties.

cell_types.name: Central Memory CD4+ T Cell
cell_types.description: Memory T cells expressing CD62L and CCR7 for lymph node homing.

cell_types.name: Effector Memory CD4+ T Cell
cell_types.description: Memory T cells lacking CCR7, ready for rapid effector function in tissues.

cell_types.name: Effector Memory CD8+ T Cell
cell_types.description: Cytotoxic memory T cells with immediate killing capacity.

cell_types.name: Tissue-Resident Memory T Cell
cell_types.description: Memory T cells permanently residing in non-lymphoid tissues.

cell_types.name: Gamma-Delta T Cell
cell_types.description: T cells with gamma-delta TCR, enriched in epithelia, bridging innate and adaptive immunity.

cell_types.name: MAIT Cell
cell_types.description: CD8+ mucosal-associated invariant T cells producing TH1 cytokines.

cell_types.name: Naive Follicular B Cell
cell_types.description: Mature B cells circulating through blood and lymph, residing in B cell follicles.

cell_types.name: Marginal Zone B Cell
cell_types.description: B cells in the splenic marginal sinus mediating T-independent antigen responses.

cell_types.name: B1 Cell
cell_types.description: Self-renewing B cells producing natural IgM against T-independent antigens.

cell_types.name: Pre-switch Memory B Cell
cell_types.description: Memory B cells that have not undergone class switching.

cell_types.name: Post-switch Memory B Cell
cell_types.description: Memory B cells that have undergone class switching to IgG or IgA.

cell_types.name: Plasmablast
cell_types.description: Recently activated B cells actively secreting antibodies but not yet fully differentiated.

cell_types.name: Plasma Cell
cell_types.description: Terminally differentiated antibody-secreting B cells.

cell_types.name: Regulatory B Cell
cell_types.description: IL-10-producing B cells that suppress immune responses.

cell_types.name: CD56dim NK Cell
cell_types.description: Major NK subset (~90%) with high CD16, potent cytotoxic activity.

cell_types.name: CD56bright NK Cell
cell_types.description: Minor NK subset (~10%) with low CD16, high cytokine production.

cell_types.name: M1 Macrophage
cell_types.description: Pro-inflammatory macrophages induced by IFN-gamma and LPS, producing TNF, IL-1, IL-6, IL-12.

cell_types.name: M2a Macrophage
cell_types.description: Alternatively activated macrophages induced by IL-4/IL-13, involved in tissue repair.

cell_types.name: M2b Macrophage
cell_types.description: Macrophages induced by immune complexes and LPS, with mixed pro/anti-inflammatory profile.

cell_types.name: M2c Macrophage
cell_types.description: Deactivated macrophages induced by IL-10 and TGF-beta, involved in immunosuppression.

cell_types.name: M2d Macrophage
cell_types.description: Tumor-associated macrophage-like cells induced by IL-6 and adenosine.

cell_types.name: Conventional CD8+ Dendritic Cell
cell_types.description: Cross-presenting DCs activating CD8+ T cells toward TH1 differentiation.

cell_types.name: Conventional CD8- Dendritic Cell
cell_types.description: DCs activating T cells toward TH2 differentiation.

cell_types.name: Plasmacytoid Dendritic Cell
cell_types.description: DC subset specializing in anti-viral immunity via IFN production.

cell_types.name: Mature Dendritic Cell
cell_types.description: Fully differentiated DCs with high antigen-presenting capacity.

cell_types.name: Embryonic Stem Cell
cell_types.description: Pluripotent stem cells derived from the inner cell mass of blastocysts.

cell_types.name: Induced Pluripotent Stem Cell
cell_types.description: Pluripotent cells generated by reprogramming somatic cells with OCT4, SOX2, KLF4, c-MYC.

cell_types.name: Cancer Stem Cell
cell_types.description: Self-renewing cells within tumors that drive tumor growth and metastasis.

cell_types.name: Neural Stem Cell
cell_types.description: Self-renewing multipotent cells generating neurons, astrocytes, and oligodendrocytes.

organs.name: Cell Lines / Other
organs.description: Established cell lines and cell types not confined to a single tissue.

microstructures.name: Miscellaneous Cell Lines
microstructures.description: Cell lines and other cell types.

cell_types.name: Jurkat Cell
cell_types.description: CD4+ T cell leukemia line used extensively in T cell signaling research.
